title: Aqarios Enters Public Markets via SPAC, Becoming Germany’s First Listed Quantum Pure-Play - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Munich-based quantum optimization software firm Aqarios GmbH went public via a SPAC reverse merger with Fonterelli SPAC 4 AG, becoming Aqarios Quantum Technologies AG and Germany's first listed pure-play quantum computing company, despite still-tiny revenue (~€1.2-1.4M/yr) as it seeks capital to scale its Luna hybrid quantum-classical platform into a recurring SaaS model.

insights.claim: Aqarios GmbH completed a reverse merger with Fonterelli SPAC 4 AG to list on the Düsseldorf Stock Exchange, renaming the combined vehicle Aqarios Quantum Technologies AG and becoming the first publicly traded pure-play quantum computing company on a German exchange.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: First European quantum-software pure-play listing gives investors a direct public equity vector into quantum optimization software rather than hardware-heavy US quantum stocks.
insights.quote: the first publicly traded pure-play quantum computing corporation on a German stock exchange

insights.claim: Aqarios is a 15-person, software-only firm spun out of LMU Munich in 2021 that builds algorithmic optimization tools for asset logistics, energy grid transmission loads, factory production planning, and investment portfolios, rather than building quantum hardware.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals a software/algorithm-layer investment thesis in quantum computing that is hardware-agnostic and can monetize before fault-tolerant quantum hardware matures.

insights.claim: Aqarios has run pilot software integrations with DAX-listed enterprises BASF, E.ON, and MTU Aero Engines, but the piece does not describe these as converted recurring commercial contracts.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Watch for conversion of these pilots into recurring SaaS contracts as the real test of commercial traction post-listing.

insights.claim: Aqarios generated annual revenue of only €1.16 million to €1.37 million across 2023-2025, with net results swinging between a €193,000 profit and a €459,000 loss due to aggressive R&D capitalization, making it a very small revenue base for a newly public company.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Investors should size position risk against the thin revenue base and volatile net income before treating the listing as a scaled commercial business.
insights.quote: generating steady annual receipts fluctuating between €1.16 million and €1.37 million across the 2023–2025 fiscal years
insights.locator: Financially, the company has operated on a combination of customer project revenues and competitive deep-tech innovation grants

insights.claim: The public listing is explicitly structured to give Aqarios the capital access and visibility needed to transition its platform from project/grant revenue into a recurring SaaS licensing business model.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The listing's stated purpose is a funding/visibility play for a business-model pivot, not a signal of current scale; track SaaS revenue disclosures post-listing.
insights.quote: the public listing is structured to provide the expanded visibility and capital access necessary to transition the platform into a recurring SaaS licensing model
insights.locator: Financially, the company has operated on a combination of customer project revenues and competitive deep-tech innovation grants

insights.claim: Aqarios' Luna platform is a hardware-agnostic abstraction middleware that lets enterprise customers route execution scripts across different quantum processor vendors without being locked into one hardware platform.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A hardware-agnostic software layer can capture enterprise value independent of which quantum hardware vendor eventually wins, reducing single-vendor technology risk for adopters.

insights.claim: Luna splits workloads by routing linear operations to classical HPC infrastructure and reserving quantum processors only for hyper-complex optimization loops, an approach designed around the current limitations of Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) hardware.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Reflects an industry-wide near-term pattern of hybrid classical-quantum architectures as a practical bridge until fault-tolerant quantum hardware arrives.
insights.quote: The platform automatically assigns linear operations to classical high-performance computing (HPC) environments, reserving the quantum processor exclusively for hyper-complex optimization loops

insights.claim: Aqarios is expanding Luna into fintech via a joint product venture with Divizend GmbH to build a quantum-accelerated portfolio optimization tool for private and institutional asset managers.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A concrete fintech go-to-market vertical to watch for early quantum-optimization revenue traction in asset management.

insights.claim: Aqarios is the core consortium lead of the Quantum Computing User Network (QuCUN), backed by a €14.2 million grant from Germany's Federal Ministry for Research, Technology, and Space (BMFTR), working alongside SAP, BASF, and LMU Munich to set open industrial quantum deployment standards across Europe.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: State-backed consortium leadership signals Aqarios is being positioned as a national-champion vector for German/European quantum computing sovereignty, which could bring follow-on public funding and standard-setting influence.
insights.locator: To build out Germany’s sovereign computing footprint

insights.claim: The newly public Aqarios Quantum Technologies AG is led by Co-Founder and CEO Michael Lachner, with a supervisory board pairing private-equity veteran Dr. Sebastian Kern (chair) with quantum academics Dr. Sebastian Feld (TU Delft) and Dr. Thomas Gabor (University of Exeter) as vice chairman and fellow.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Board composition blending capital-markets credibility with technical quantum-architecture expertise suggests an intent to reassure public investors on both financial governance and scientific legitimacy.
insights.locator: The newly public entity is led by Co-Founder and CEO Michael Lachner
